title: In the midst of the MVP race, Jaylen Brown explains his stance on the NBA’s 65-game rule
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: Jaylen Brown expressed support for the NBA's 65-game rule but suggested 62 games as a preferred threshold.
Cade Cunningham may miss MVP contention due to falling short of the 65-game requirement after a lung injury.
Jaylen Brown aims to play his 65th game, potentially becoming the first top-five MVP candidate to meet the requirement.

title: ‘It felt like a gut punch’: Pride flags removed from BU offices ignite free speech, inclusion debate
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Boston University removed Pride flags from three campus locations, citing a 'content-neutral' signage policy banning outward-facing displays.
Faculty and students argue the removal undermines free expression and LGBTQ+ visibility, sparking campus protests and letters to BU officials.
Critics claim inconsistent policy enforcement, as other external displays remain, raising legal questions under Massachusetts Civil Rights Act.

title: David Ortiz won Boston.com’s bracket of the best sports soundbites, and it wasn’t close
author: Hayden Bird
bullets: David Ortiz's 'This is our f****** city' won Boston.com's 2026 bracket for best Boston sports soundbite.
Ortiz's quote from the 2013 Boston Marathon memorial service received the most votes, including 2,796 in the first round.
Kevin Millar's 'Don’t let us win today' advanced to the final, marking a significant upset in the competition.

title: A JetBlue flight reportedly hit a coyote during takeoff at T.F. Green Airport
author: Kristi Palma
bullets: A JetBlue flight hit a coyote during takeoff at T.F. Green Airport on March 24.
The aircraft returned to the airport for inspection, landing safely without reported issues.
The flight subsequently continued to JFK International Airport, and the runway remained open.

title: Boston runner to take on 2026 Marathon for Corey C. Griffin Foundation
author: Kelly Chan
bullets: Annie Yardley, 30, will run the 2026 Boston Marathon for the Corey C. Griffin Foundation.
Yardley has transitioned from non-athlete to marathon runner, inspired by the foundation's work with children.
The Corey C. Griffin Foundation supports education and healthcare, helping kids envision broader futures.

title: Cape Cod man charged with sister’s murder
author: John Waller
bullets: A 67-year-old man, Kenneth Beltrame, is charged with murdering his 61-year-old sister, Jennifer Beltrame, in Sandwich, MA.
Kenneth Beltrame is scheduled for arraignment in Barnstable District Court after police found his sister's body in their home.
Jennifer Beltrame's body showed signs of violent trauma; the investigation is ongoing by Sandwich Police and State Police detectives.

title: No one can use a gas-powered leaf blower in these 2 Mass. towns, thanks to new laws
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Arlington and Lexington towns have banned gas-powered leaf blowers, allowing only electric models due to noise and environmental concerns.
Limited exceptions allow wheeled leaf blowers with four-stroke engines on large properties and gas models during emergencies.
Violations in Arlington and Lexington incur fines up to $300, with complaint-based enforcement by town health and police departments.

title: Fenway Park offers 4 new food concessions, including lobster poutine
author: Katelyn Umholtz
bullets: Fenway Park introduces Lobstah Poutine with Luke’s Lobster, bacon, and clam chowder for the 2026 season.
New food items include Surf & Turf Dog with Kobe beef, Luke's Lobster, and Savenor's bacon.
Mini empanadas and spicy grilled cheese sandwiches are also added to Fenway Park's concessions menu.

title: 3 arrested in ‘highly coordinated’ break-in scheme that targeted Indian, East Asian residents
author: Abby Patkin
bullets: Three men arrested in Woburn, Massachusetts, for a coordinated break-in scheme targeting Indian and East Asian residents.
David Rondon-Castro, Yeison Ramirez-Garcia, and Itan Dami Beltran used GPS trackers, hidden cameras, and Wi-Fi jammers.
The crime ring stole hundreds of thousands in cash, jewelry, and heirlooms, though the valuables haven't been recovered.

title: Here’s where experts rank the Red Sox entering the 2026 season
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: The Boston Red Sox are ranked between No. 7 and No. 9 in various 2026 MLB preseason power rankings.
The Red Sox's new starting rotation features Sonny Gray and Ranger Suárez, and is considered a team strength.
ESPN projects Roman Anthony as an MVP candidate, citing his performance in the World Baseball Classic.
